docs.title: GitHub Portfolio System (GPS)
docs.text: Welcome to the official developer portal for the GitHub Portfolio System (GPS). GPS is a production-grade framework engineered to automate, standardize, and scale your personal coding portfolio and repository governance models.

docs.title: 🗺️ Documentation Directory
docs.text:
Use the cards below to navigate the guides:

🏗️ System Architecture

Explore data pipeline flows, component decouple models, and Mermaid schematic diagrams.

⚙️ Setup Guide

Step-by-step instructions to clone the workspace, establish keys, and run validation gates.

🚀 CI/CD Workflows

Detailed guide on automated metrics calendars, contribution snake games, and CodeQL checks.

🌐 Deployment Guide

How to compile the static site and launch your documentation on GitHub Pages.

🔮 Future Roadmap

Integration pathways for Hugging Face datasets, Kaggle stats, and the AetherOS ecosystem.

docs.title: 🚀 Design Philosophy
docs.text: GPS is built to serve three core values: * Truthful Portfolio: Focuses on real, high-impact project architectures (like custom YOLO decoupled layers) rather than decorative badge padding. * Automated Updates: Restores time by keeping stats, Streaks, and active repositories in sync automatically. * Clean Design: Built with complete dark/light theme support, accessible contrasting colors, and clean grid components.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: GPS exposes a modular Python API for programmatic execution.

docs.title: System Architecture
docs.text: This document describes the high-level architecture, software layers, components, and data flows of the GitHub Portfolio System (GPS).

docs.title: 1. System Overview
docs.text:
GPS is designed as a modular developer platform. It decouples your portfolio content (raw data, bios, project files) from the delivery mechanisms (GitHub Profiles, Portfolio Websites, and GitHub Pages). 

graph TD
    subgraph Development Layer
        Src[automation/update_readme.py]
        Tests[tests/test_validation.py]
    end

    subgraph Automation Layer
        GHA[GitHub Actions Workflows]
        Snake[snake.yml]
        Metrics[metrics.yml]
        Readme[readme.yml]
    end

    subgraph Presentation Layer
        ProfileReadme[profile/README.md]
        DocsSite[Docs site via GitHub Pages]
        RepoTemplates[templates/repo_readme_template.md]
    end

    subgraph External Sources
        GitHubAPI[GitHub API]
        WakaTime[WakaTime API]
    end

    Src -->|Fetches Data| GitHubAPI
    Src -->|Fetches Data| WakaTime
    Readme -->|Runs| Src
    Readme -->|Updates| ProfileReadme
    Snake -->|Generates SVG| ProfileReadme
    Metrics -->|Generates Stats SVG| ProfileReadme
    GHA -->|Deploys docs/| DocsSite

docs.title: Presentation Layer
docs.text:
• Profile README (profile/README.md): The entry-point for visitors of the GitHub profile. Aggregates stats, automated graphics, and bio sections.
  • Documentation Site (docs/): A complete documentation website configured via MkDocs and hosted on GitHub Pages.
  • Repository Templates (templates/): Standardization templates that ensure all software projects inherit consistent styling, headers, and contributing parameters.

docs.title: Automation Layer
docs.text:
• Update Engine (automation/update_readme.py): A Python-based automation controller that calls API endpoints to retrieve language usage, active repository descriptions, and contributions.
  • Cron Schedules: Orchestrated via GitHub Actions (.github/workflows/) to run daily updates, keeping stats clean and updated without manual intervention.
  • Security & Health Checkers: Dependabot and CodeQL keep dependencies secure and ensure python automation scripts do not leak tokens.

docs.title: Branding Layer
docs.text: • Brand Guidelines (branding/): Colors, font families, and SVG layout criteria ensuring light and dark themes render cleanly across devices.

docs.title: 3. Technology Stack
docs.text:
• Logic & Scripts: Python 3.10+ (using urllib, json, and unittest) for minimal external dependencies and quick startup times in workflows.
  • Static Site Generator: MkDocs with the Material theme for modern, high-contrast, responsive documentation.
  • Workflows: GitHub Actions YAML config.
  • Visualization: GitHub Readme Metrics (SVG templates) and Platane Contribution Snake (dynamic action).

docs.title: CI/CD Workflows
docs.text: This document describes the automated integration and deployment workflows configured in the system.

docs.title: 1. Overview of Workflows
docs.text:
The automation in GPS is managed through YAML configuration files located in workflows.

Workflow Name Trigger Frequency Purpose Contribution Snake Daily (Cron) & Push Generates a 3D/2D grid animation of contribution history. Profile Metrics Daily (Cron) & Push Renders languages, achievements, and code stats. README Updater Hourly/Daily (Cron) Runs the Python automation script to update WakaTime/repository logs. CodeQL Security Analysis Weekly & PR Code vulnerability scanning. Release & Changelog Push to main automates release tagging and updates CHANGELOG.md.

docs.title: 2. CI Quality Gates
docs.text:
Every pull request runs standard validation checks: 1. Format Check: Ensures Markdown files adhere to structural standards. 2. Linting: Runs checks on GitHub Actions syntax. 3. Unit Tests: Runs the tests/test_validation.py script.

If any check fails, merging is blocked to maintain repository health.

docs.title: 3. Customizing the Actions
docs.text:
To alter the schedule or output directories of the SVGs, edit the respective workflow file. For instance, in .github/workflows/snake.yml, you can customize the colors of the snake:

- name: generate-snake-game-from-github-contribution-grid
  uses: platane/snk@v3
  with:
    github_user_name: ${{ github.repository_owner }}
    outputs: |
      dist/github-contribution-grid-snake.svg
      dist/github-contribution-grid-snake-dark.svg?palette=github-dark

docs.title: Contributing Guide
docs.text: Contributions are welcome! Please follow these steps to set up and develop the GPS platform.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/Adithshajee/github-portfolio-system.git
cd github-portfolio-system
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: Running Checks
docs.text:
ruff check src/ tests/
mypy src/gps/
pytest tests/

For more info, read the root CONTRIBUTING.md file.

docs.title: Deployment Guide
docs.text: This guide details how to host and deploy the GitHub Portfolio System documentation site via GitHub Pages.

docs.title: 1. Local Preview
docs.text:
Before deploying, always test the site layout locally:

# Start local MkDocs dev server
mkdocs serve

Once running, navigate to http://127.0.0.1:8000 in your web browser to check pages, navigation, search capabilities, and CSS styles.

docs.title: 2. GitHub Pages Deployment
docs.text: We use a automated GitHub Actions workflow to compile the site and push it to the gh-pages branch.

docs.title: Automated Workflow Configuration
docs.text: The release and deployment action performs the following steps: 1. Checks out the code. 2. Sets up Python environment. 3. Installs dependencies (mkdocs and mkdocs-material). 4. Builds the static site using mkdocs build. 5. Deploys the static assets to the gh-pages branch.

docs.title: Manual Branch Deployment
docs.text:
If you prefer to deploy manually from your terminal, execute:

mkdocs gh-deploy --clean

docs.title: 3. Configuring Repository Settings
docs.text: To ensure the site resolves correctly on GitHub: 1. Go to the repository on GitHub. 2. Click on Settings > Pages (in the sidebar). 3. Set the Source to Deploy from a branch. 4. Choose the branch gh-pages and folder / (root). 5. Save your changes. The site will be available shortly at https://<your-username>.github.io/github-portfolio-system/.

docs.title: Architecture Decision Records (ADR)
docs.text: This page lists key architectural and design decisions made during the v2.0 development cycle.

docs.title: ADR-001: Provider Plugin System
docs.text:
• Decision: Implement a registry pattern where providers inherit from BaseProvider and use @register decorator.
  • Consequence: Easy to add new developer profile integrations without modifying GPSEngine core loops.

docs.title: ADR-002: Pydantic v2 Core Config
docs.text:
• Decision: Utilize Pydantic Models for settings loading and API schema translation.
  • Consequence: Strong types, fail-fast validations, and strict type casting for timezone-aware date parsing.

docs.title: Q: Why is my profile README not updating?
docs.text:
• Ensure the start/end markers <!-- REPOS_START --> and <!-- REPOS_END --> exist in your README.
  • Check that the readme_path in gps.yml matches your file location.
  • Verify GH_PAT token is loaded in the terminal or action environment.

docs.title: Q: How can I change the number of repositories listed?
docs.text:
Modify providers.github.repo_count inside gps.yml:

providers:
  github:
    enabled: true
    repo_count: 10

docs.title: Future Roadmap
docs.text: This document outlines the planned enhancements and future integration vectors for the GitHub Portfolio System (GPS).

docs.title: 🗺️ Execution Phases
docs.text:
gantt
    title GPS Feature Roadmap (2026)
    dateFormat  YYYY-MM
    section Phase 1
    Foundation & Profile Setup     :active, p1, 2026-07, 1m
    section Phase 2
    Automated Metrics & Actions    :active, p2, 2026-07, 1m
    section Phase 3
    External Portfolios Sync       :    p3, 2026-08, 2m
    section Phase 4
    AetherOS Core Integration     :    p4, 2026-10, 3m

docs.title: Phase 1: Core System (Current)
docs.text:
• Recruiter-Grade README: Full integration of metrics, stats, banners, and layout grids.
  • Standardized Automation: Git contribution snake and daily metrics.
  • Documentation Site: MkDocs structure deployed to GitHub Pages.

docs.title: Phase 2: External Platform Syncing (Q3 2026)
docs.text:
• Hugging Face Sync: Auto-fetch uploaded transformer models, space statuses, and datasets to display on the GitHub profile.
  • Kaggle Metrics API: Dynamically list competitive score rankings, notebook views, and gold medals.
  • LinkedIn Webhook: Sync work history and achievements from LinkedIn directly.

docs.title: Phase 3: AetherOS Core Integration (Q4 2026)
docs.text:
• Documentation Host: Scale the documentation site to host the official guides and APIs for AetherOS.
  • Build Pipeline Check: Integrate GitHub Actions validation checks to ensure AetherOS OS-level builds compile successfully across platforms.
  • Docker Hub Integration: Automate Docker image builds of the AetherOS simulator.

docs.title: Security Policy
docs.text: Please read our security guidelines and standard procedures.

docs.title: Reporting a Vulnerability
docs.text:
Please do NOT open a public issue for security findings. Instead: - Open a private advisory on GitHub Security panel. - Or email adideva12345@gmail.com with [GPS Security] prefix.

We will respond within 48 hours and release fixes within 7 days.

See SECURITY.md for more details.

docs.title: Setup Guide
docs.text: Follow this guide to set up the GitHub Portfolio System (GPS) for your personal profile.

docs.title: Prerequisites
docs.text: Before configuring GPS, ensure you have: 1. A GitHub Account with a repository named <your-username> (if deploying as your main profile README) or github-portfolio-system. 2. Python 3.10 or higher installed locally. 3. An optional WakaTime account (if you wish to track coding stats).

docs.title: 1. Local Initialization
docs.text:
Clone this repository to your local system:

git clone https://github.com/Adithshajee/github-portfolio-system.git
cd github-portfolio-system

Install local development and static documentation requirements:

pip install -r requirements.txt

docs.title: 2. Configuring Secrets on GitHub
docs.text:
To support the automation engine (such as updating WakaTime statistics or pulling metrics graphs), configure the following repository secrets:

  1. Navigate to your repository on GitHub.
  2. Go to Settings > Secrets and variables > Actions.
  3. Click New repository secret.
  4. Add the following secrets:
    • WAKATIME_API_KEY: Your private WakaTime API key (found in WakaTime account settings).
    • GH_PAT (Optional): A Personal Access Token with repo scopes if you want to pull statistics from private repositories.

docs.title: 3. Local Verification
docs.text:
To run a dry run of the automation scripts locally:

python automation/update_readme.py --dry-run

To run the validation test suite:

python -m unittest tests/test_validation.py

docs.title: Troubleshooting Guide
docs.text: Here are common issues and error resolutions.

docs.title: 1. RateLimitError: Rate limit exceeded
docs.text: Cause: The GitHub API limit of 60 requests per hour for unauthenticated users has been exceeded. Fix: Set the GH_PAT environment variable with a GitHub Personal Access Token to increase the limit to 5000 requests per hour.

docs.title: 2. Invalid syntax errors during installation
docs.text:
Cause: Using an outdated Python version (GPS requires Python >= 3.10) or setuptools cache. Fix: Upgrade Python to 3.10+ and install package dependencies in a virtual environment: 

python -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e ".[dev]"

docs.title: GitHub Provider
docs.text: The GitHub provider is active by default. It parses user profile data using GitHub REST v3 API and pinned repositories using GitHub GraphQL v4 API.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Enable or tune options in your gps.yml file:

providers:
  github:
    enabled: true
    repo_count: 5
    include_pinned: true
    exclude_forks: true
    exclude_archived: true

docs.title: Secrets
docs.text:
To fetch pinned repositories via GraphQL or prevent API rate limits, set the GH_PAT environment variable with a Personal Access Token:

export GH_PAT=ghp_your_token_value

docs.title: Hugging Face Provider
docs.text: The Hugging Face provider fetches model cards, spaces, and datasets owned by the configured user.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Enable it in your gps.yml file:

providers:
  huggingface:
    enabled: true
    username: "your-hf-username"
    model_count: 5
    space_count: 3

docs.title: Secrets
docs.text:
Provide HF_TOKEN environment variable for authenticated access or to view private models:

export HF_TOKEN=hf_your_token_value

docs.title: Kaggle Provider
docs.text: The Kaggle provider integration utilizes the official Kaggle API package to aggregate public competitions, notebooks, and dataset profiles.

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text:
Enable it in your gps.yml file:

providers:
  kaggle:
    enabled: true
    username: "your-kaggle-username"

docs.title: Secrets
docs.text:
Requires your API Credentials loaded in the environment:

export KAGGLE_USERNAME="your-kaggle-username"
export KAGGLE_KEY="your-kaggle-api-key"
